text:
Once upon an evening moonlit sky…

There fluttered an old-fashioned letter tucked onto my writing desk. My favorite feather owl flew excitedly around me. I knew.

Opening parchment—I think?

Yes—but my eagle eyes.

There?

A fox?

A letter!

A letter! Surely?

A letter loves?

I imagine fox loves.

Well. Look!

You think?

Why yes?

I think?

Well?




Why?













Because loves.

Because loves?


That’s silly.




Thought?


Thought?


That fox loves.

Aww.



Aren’t foxes lovely?

Yes?

Oh!?


Well!

Yes?







Oh.


How strange.

Yes.


Oh think?

Of course?

Oh think?

That?

Something.

Something loves?

Something loves me?


Why?







Just thinking.

Just think?

Just feather?

Just feather love?

That?

You love?

Yes…I think.



That fox loves me. Thoughtful.


That fox loves feather.

Ah.

Yes.



Thoughtful loving feather?

Whew
